internal_issues.description: Mount Olympus listed as cosmological axis-mundi alongside Meru/Qaf in detail_evidence argument (2) — category-blurring. Olympus in Greek myth is where the gods live, not a geometrically-positioned cosmic axis. Mirrors the category error we accuse the dome of.

suggested_patch.find: (Mount Meru is north of a spherical earth in Hindu cosmology; Yggdrasil connects nine realms with no single spatial axis; Mount Olympus is at 40°N in Greek geography; Mount Qaf encircles the boundary with no interior center)
suggested_patch.replace: (Mount Meru is at the center of a flat disc in Puranic Hindu cosmology, or at the geographic north pole of a spherical earth in the Aryabhatan astronomical tradition; Yggdrasil is a vertical world-tree connecting nine realms across three tiers with no specified earth-geometry beneath it; Mount Qaf encircles the world's boundary with no interior center; the Mesopotamian Ḫursag / E-kur stands at the cosmic center of a flat-earth model)

internal_issues.description: Yggdrasil described as having 'no single spatial axis' — opposite of true. Yggdrasil IS the vertical world-tree axis. Fixed in combined patch with ISS-1817.
Meru characterized as 'north of a spherical earth' (Aryabhatan minority tradition). Dominant Puranic tradition places Meru at center of flat disc. Fixed in combined patch.
detail_verdict_text: 'centuries to millennia' inconsistent with detail_evidence/tldr_verdict 'two to three millennia'. Fixed to 'two to three millennia'.
Carve-out clause didn't name specific fraction. Added '(66/70 = 94.3% if WIN-031, 032, and 034 are all carved out)' to close rhetorical gap.
detail_verdict_text monitor.py active/defined ordering clarified: '28 active domains against status_history.json; 39 defined at commit 8e4bf9b' (active count leads). Carried from c4.
Em-dash style in detail_evidence monitor.py parenthetical. Closed stale — em-dash no longer present in current text (already uses colon). Prior patch resolved this.
